meta.name: ERQ Emotion Regulation Questionnaire
meta.description: The ERQ is a 10-item scale assessing two emotion regulation strategies: cognitive reappraisal (changing how one thinks about a situation to alter its emotional impact) and expressive suppression (inhibiting emotional expression). Cognitive reappraisal is generally considered more adaptive, while excessive use of expressive suppression may be associated with negative mental health outcomes.
meta.author: Gross & John (2003)
meta.reference: Gross JJ, John OP. Individual differences in two emotion regulation processes: implications for affect, relationships, and well-being. J Pers Soc Psychol. 2003;85(2):348-362.
meta.instruction: We would like to ask you some questions about your emotional life, in particular, how you control and regulate your emotions. Please rate the extent to which you agree with each statement.

questions.text: When I want to feel more positive emotion (such as joy or amusement), I change what I am thinking about
I keep my emotions to myself
When I want to feel less negative emotion (such as sadness or anger), I change what I am thinking about
When I am feeling positive emotions, I am careful not to express them
When I am faced with a stressful situation, I make myself think about it in a way that helps me stay calm
I control my emotions by not expressing them
When I want to feel more positive emotion, I change the way I am thinking about the situation
I control my emotions by changing the way I think about the situation I am in
When I am feeling negative emotions, I make sure not to express them
When I want to feel less negative emotion, I change the way I am thinking about the situation

reappraisal.label: Low
reappraisal.description: You rarely use cognitive reappraisal. This adaptive strategy, when underused, may limit emotional regulation flexibility. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

reappraisal.label: Average
reappraisal.description: You use cognitive reappraisal at a moderate frequency. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

reappraisal.label: High
reappraisal.description: You frequently use cognitive reappraisal, which is associated with better emotional well-being and interpersonal relationships. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suppression.label: Low
suppression.description: You rarely suppress emotional expression. Moderate emotional expression is beneficial for relationships and mental health. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suppression.label: Average
suppression.description: You use expressive suppression at a moderate frequency. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

suppression.label: High
suppression.description: You frequently suppress emotional expression. Excessive suppression may be associated with lower well-being and social support. Consider learning more flexible emotion regulation approaches.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
